phase3_findings.description: no negative control that a successful (rc==0) call whose output contains the bad-cred signature does NOT get the DIAG line (unpinned [ rc -ne 0 ] conjunct)
real_mint 'no private key on stdin' guard uncovered
stop-refresher defeat warning over-claims git push stale on a surface-2-only divergence
arm19 relies on a reaped PID not being reused (flaky + innocent-kill risk)
surface-2 mv rename-failure branch untested (message-pinned only)

fix_decisions.claim_text: rc-0 negative control missing for the DIAG-append guard
fix_decisions.evidence: over-grade shape 1/2 (fail-closed/diagnostic-only): the guarded regression appends only a spurious advisory DIAG line on a HEALTHY call; the two-strikes rule keys on failures so it cannot drive a false abort. Single-source from pr-test-analyzer (empirical over-grader). Fixed anyway (arm30).

mv-failure-with-temp-write-success is not deterministically forceable at unit scale (rename within the same writable dir succeeds); the branch is a near-duplicate of the tested temp-write branch and its divergence message is already pinned by arm4d-pin (count 2). Re-raised by convergence pr-test-analyzer; still deferred with the same reasoning.

fix_decisions.claim_text: two-strikes prose keys on bare 'Authentication failed' while gh-fresh.sh SIG anchors to 'fatal: Authentication failed for'
fix_decisions.evidence: The prose faithfully implements issue #487's fail-fast AC, which explicitly specifies the bare signature (HTTP 401 / Bad credentials / Authentication failed) and notes git push failures are covered by the prose alone (git's real message 'fatal: Authentication failed for' matches the bare form). The wrapper's anchoring is a deliberate wrapper-specific refinement to avoid a false auto-DIAG on gh's own output (proven by arm26b); the two layers are intentionally distinct. The convergence final-pass reviewer independently corroborated this ('correct as-is; noting it only so a future editor doesn't reconcile them and accidentally re-widen the wrapper'). Aligning the prose would deviate from the AC.

notes: Promoted iteration from the engine_self_modifying early shadow. Merged origin/main (16 commits incl. #495/#489) cleanly — no conflicts (post-merge suite 7229/0). Convergence-time shadow ran on HEAD f1c5e5db with FULL coverage (5/5 reviewers); no new Critical/Important; new prose-signature Suggestion deferred as spec-faithful (corroborated by final-pass); surface-2 mv + arm28 are known deferrals. Post-shadow edit gate: HEAD unchanged, shadow reviewed exactly what ships.

phase3_findings.description: prose two-strikes signature (bare) vs wrapper anchored SIG
